questions.question: A student uses a measuring cylinder to measure the volume of some water. The diagram shows part of the measuring cylinder. The top and bottom of the meniscus are labelled. 50 45 40 top of meniscus bottom of meniscus cm 3 What is the volume of the water? A 47.0 cm 3, B 47.5 cm 3, C 49.0 cm 3, D 49.5 cm 3
questions.text: A student uses a measuring cylinder to measure the volume of some water. The diagram shows part of the measuring cylinder. The top and bottom of the meniscus are labelled. 50 45 40 top of meniscus bottom of meniscus cm 3 What is the volume of the water? A 47.0 cm 3, B 47.5 cm 3, C 49.0 cm 3, D 49.5 cm 3

questions.question: The graph represents the motion of a train travelling between two stations. 0 0 100   200   300   400   500 20 10 600   700   800   900 speed m / s time / s Which statement about the train is correct? A Its acceleration takes a longer time than its deceleration., B It travels at constant speed for less than half of its journey time., C It travels 2000 m in the first 100 s., D It travels 10 000 m at constant speed. 3
questions.text: The graph represents the motion of a train travelling between two stations. 0 0 100   200   300   400   500 20 10 600   700   800   900 speed m / s time / s Which statement about the train is correct? A Its acceleration takes a longer time than its deceleration., B It travels at constant speed for less than half of its journey time., C It travels 2000 m in the first 100 s., D It travels 10 000 m at constant speed. 3
options: A Its acceleration takes a longer time than its deceleration.
B It travels at constant speed for less than half of its journey time.
C It travels 2000 m in the first 100 s.
D It travels 10 000 m at constant speed. 3

questions.question: A car travels 6.0 km along a main road in 6.0 minutes. It then travels 2.0 km along a minor road in 6.0 minutes. main road minor road 6.0 km 6.0 minutes 2.0 km 6.0 minutes Which calculation of average speed for the whole journey is correct? A 8.0 ÷ 12.0 = 0.67 km / minute, B 12.0 ÷ 8.0 = 1.5 km / minute, C 8.0 + 12.0 = 20 km / minute, D 8.0 × 12.0 = 96 km / minute
questions.text: A car travels 6.0 km along a main road in 6.0 minutes. It then travels 2.0 km along a minor road in 6.0 minutes. main road minor road 6.0 km 6.0 minutes 2.0 km 6.0 minutes Which calculation of average speed for the whole journey is correct? A 8.0 ÷ 12.0 = 0.67 km / minute, B 12.0 ÷ 8.0 = 1.5 km / minute, C 8.0 + 12.0 = 20 km / minute, D 8.0 × 12.0 = 96 km / minute

questions.question: Two metal blocks P and Q have identical dimensions. They hang on identical spring balances. 0 1 2 3 4 6 5 N 0 1 2 3 4 6 5 N P Q What can be deduced about P and Q? A They have different volumes and different weights., B They have different volumes, but equal masses., C They have equal volumes and equal weights., D They have equal volumes, but different masses. 4
questions.text: Two metal blocks P and Q have identical dimensions. They hang on identical spring balances. 0 1 2 3 4 6 5 N 0 1 2 3 4 6 5 N P Q What can be deduced about P and Q? A They have different volumes and different weights., B They have different volumes, but equal masses., C They have equal volumes and equal weights., D They have equal volumes, but different masses. 4
options: A They have different volumes and different weights.
B They have different volumes, but equal masses.
C They have equal volumes and equal weights.
D They have equal volumes, but different masses. 4

questions.question: A person measures the length, width, height and mass of a metal block with rectangular sides. Which of these measurements must be used in order to calculate the density of the metal? A mass   only, B height and mass only, C length, width and height only, D length, width, height and mass
questions.text: A person measures the length, width, height and mass of a metal block with rectangular sides. Which of these measurements must be used in order to calculate the density of the metal? A mass   only, B height and mass only, C length, width and height only, D length, width, height and mass
options: C length, width and height only
D length, width, height and mass

questions.question: The diagram shows the only three forces acting on an object. 5.0 N 3.0 N 2.0 N What is the resultant force on the object? A 0 N, B 5.0 N towards the left, C 5.0 N towards the right, D 10.0 N towards the right

questions.question: A uniform rod rests on a pivot at its centre. The rod is not attached to the pivot. Forces are then applied to the rod in four different ways, as shown. The weight of the rod can be ignored. Which diagram shows the rod in equilibrium? A 100 N 100 N B 100 N100 N C 100 N100 N D 100 N100 N 100 N 5

questions.question: A helicopter takes off from the ground and rises vertically. It then hovers at a constant height above the ground. Which sequence of energy changes takes place during the gain in height? A chemical → gravitational potential → kinetic, B chemical → kinetic → gravitational potential, C gravitational potential → chemical → kinetic, D kinetic → chemical → gravitational potential
questions.text: A helicopter takes off from the ground and rises vertically. It then hovers at a constant height above the ground. Which sequence of energy changes takes place during the gain in height? A chemical → gravitational potential → kinetic, B chemical → kinetic → gravitational potential, C gravitational potential → chemical → kinetic, D kinetic → chemical → gravitational potential

questions.question: Four people of equal weight on a beach use different routes to get to the top of a sea wall. beach slipway ladder sea wall Which person produces the greatest average power? person                   route                    time                   taken / s A runs across the beach, then climbs the ladder 8, B walks across the beach, then climbs the ladder 16, C runs up the slipway 5, D walks up the slipway 10
questions.text: Four people of equal weight on a beach use different routes to get to the top of a sea wall. beach slipway ladder sea wall Which person produces the greatest average power? person                   route                    time                   taken / s A runs across the beach, then climbs the ladder 8, B walks across the beach, then climbs the ladder 16, C runs up the slipway 5, D walks up the slipway 10
options: A runs across the beach, then climbs the ladder 8
B walks across the beach, then climbs the ladder 16

questions.question: A vehicle sinks into soft ground. The vehicle is changed so that it does not sink as far. Which change is made? A a lower centre of mass, B a more powerful engine, C wheels that are further apart, D wider tyres 6
questions.text: A vehicle sinks into soft ground. The vehicle is changed so that it does not sink as far. Which change is made? A a lower centre of mass, B a more powerful engine, C wheels that are further apart, D wider tyres 6
options: A a lower centre of mass
C wheels that are further apart

questions.text: A manometer is used to measure the pressure of a gas trapped in a cylinder. At which labelled point on the diagram is the pressure greatest? gas cylinder AC D B

questions.question: Extremely small pollen grains in water are viewed through a microscope. The grains are seen to move continually and randomly. What is the reason for this random movement? A The grains are moved by randomly moving water molecules., B The grains are moved by random convection currents in the water., C The grains are moved by random rays of light reflecting off them., D The grains are moved by the random motion of their own atoms.
questions.text: Extremely small pollen grains in water are viewed through a microscope. The grains are seen to move continually and randomly. What is the reason for this random movement? A The grains are moved by randomly moving water molecules., B The grains are moved by random convection currents in the water., C The grains are moved by random rays of light reflecting off them., D The grains are moved by the random motion of their own atoms.
options: A The grains are moved by randomly moving water molecules.
B The grains are moved by random convection currents in the water.
C The grains are moved by random rays of light reflecting off them.
D The grains are moved by the random motion of their own atoms.

questions.question: A gas is compressed in a sealed cylinder by moving a piston. gas initial position piston gas after gas has been compressed piston Which row in the table states what happens to the density of the gas and to the pressure of the gas when it is compressed? density         pressure A decreases       decreases, B decreases       increases, C increases       decreases, D increases        increases 7

questions.question: When steam condenses it becomes liquid water. When liquid water solidifies it becomes ice. What  happens  to  the  temperature  of  steam  while  it  is  condensing,  and  what  happens  to  the temperature of water while it is solidifying? temperature of steam while it is condensing temperature of water while it is solidifying A decreases            decreases, B decreases stays the same, C stays the same decreases, D stays the same stays the same
questions.text: When steam condenses it becomes liquid water. When liquid water solidifies it becomes ice. What  happens  to  the  temperature  of  steam  while  it  is  condensing,  and  what  happens  to  the temperature of water while it is solidifying? temperature of steam while it is condensing temperature of water while it is solidifying A decreases            decreases, B decreases stays the same, C stays the same decreases, D stays the same stays the same
options: D stays the same stays the same

questions.question: A thermometer has graduations which start at –10 °C and end at 110 °C. –10   0100  110 °C What is the lower fixed point and what is the upper fixed point of the Celsius scale? lower fixed point / °C upper fixed point / °C A –10              100, B –10              110, C 0              100, D 0              110

questions.question: Ice is trapped by a metal gauze at the bottom of a tube containing water. The water is heated strongly at the top, but the ice only melts very slowly. heat water boiling metal gauze ice Why does the ice melt so slowly? A Heat energy always travels upwards., B Hot water is more dense than cold water., C Metal gauze does not allow heat to pass through., D Water is a poor conductor of heat.
questions.text: Ice is trapped by a metal gauze at the bottom of a tube containing water. A Heat energy always travels upwards., B Hot water is more dense than cold water., C Metal gauze does not allow heat to pass through., D Water is a poor conductor of heat.
options: A Heat energy always travels upwards.
B Hot water is more dense than cold water.
C Metal gauze does not allow heat to pass through.
D Water is a poor conductor of heat.

questions.question: The diagram shows a water wave passing through a gap in a harbour wall. The wavefronts curve round the wall and reach a small boat in the harbour. boat harbour wall harbou r wavefront gap What is the name of this curving effect, and how can the gap be changed so that the wavefronts do not reach the boat? name of effect change to the gap A diffraction make the gap slightly bigger, B diffraction make the gap slightly smaller, C refraction make the gap slightly bigger, D refraction make the gap slightly smaller

options: A diffraction make the gap slightly bigger
B diffraction make the gap slightly smaller
C refraction make the gap slightly bigger
D refraction make the gap slightly smaller

A The angle of incidence at mirror X is 30°., B The angle of incidence at mirror Y is 60°., C The angle of reflection at mirror X is 120°., D The angle of reflection at mirror Y is 0°.

questions.question: A teacher wishes to show the production of electrostatic charges. She holds a rod and rubs it with a cotton cloth. A copper rod, a glass rod, a plastic rod and a steel rod are available. Which two rods would both be suitable to use? A a copper rod and a glass rod, B a glass rod and a plastic rod, C a plastic rod and a copper rod, D a plastic rod and a steel rod

B a glass rod and a plastic rod
